articles.title: If you're building an AI business, achieving ISO 42001 compliance
articles.summary: shows your customers that you are taking the necessary steps to ensure responsible usage and development of AI. But this can cost you real time and  money.

articles.title: INTRODUCING TOGETHER CHAT: USE DEEPSEEK R1 FOR FREE, HOSTED IN NORTH AMERICA
articles.summary: Together Chat has launched a free consumer app that offers seamless interaction with top open-source models, including the new DeepSeek R1 for advanced reasoning. It supports web crawling, code generation with Qwen Coder 32B, and image creation using Flux Schnell, all accessible via a Progressive Web App.

articles.title: ROBLOX'S NEW AI MODEL CAN GENERATE 3D OBJECTS
articles.summary: Roblox has open-sourced Cube 3D, an AI model that can generate 3D objects from text prompts, to enhance creation efficiency. Cube 3D uses tokenization techniques and is trained with licensed and publicly available datasets, plus Roblox experience data. Future iterations will incorporate multimodal inputs like images and videos.

articles.title: NEW REVE IMAGE GENERATOR BEATS AI ART HEAVYWEIGHTS MIDJOURNEY AND FLUX AT A PENNY PER IMAGE
articles.summary: Reve Image 1.0, an AI image generator, offers competitive pricing and impressive prompt adherence, realism, and versatility, potentially outperforming rivals like Midjourney and Ideogram. At $5 for 500 credits, it allows affordable generation of high-quality images but lacks an edit feature and mobile app, which may deter advanced users. Despite limited transparency about its team and technology, Reve remains a strong cost-effective choice for users seeking high-quality outputs without extensive technical know-how.

articles.title: LLAVA-MORE
articles.summary: LLaVA-MORE systematically evaluates different language models and visual backbones in multimodal LLMs, providing a reproducible framework for comparing architectures.

articles.title: SKELETON-BASED ACTION SEGMENTATION
articles.summary: TRG-Net enhances action segmentation using text-derived relational graphs for improved spatial-temporal modeling and supervision.

articles.title: HIERARCHICAL DOCUMENT STRUCTURE ANALYSIS
articles.summary: UniHDSA is a unified relation prediction approach for hierarchical document structure analysis, enabling a single module to handle multiple tasks simultaneously.

articles.title: SINGLE IMAGE PERSONALIZATION WITHOUT TRAINING
articles.summary: SISO enables training-free personalization for image generation and editing by optimizing similarity loss iteratively.

articles.title: AWESOME MRI RECONSTRUCTION
articles.summary: A curated collection of articles about deep learning in MRI reconstruction.

articles.title: OPEN-SOURCE OMNI-MODAL FOUNDATION MODEL
articles.summary: The Baichuan Omni 1.5 model supports text, image, video, and audio inputs as well as text and audio outputs. This means it is another example of an any-to-any style model, sometimes referred to as natively multimodal. The model, like many, uses an interleaved multimodal tokens approach where different types of tokens are routed to different encoders/decoders that all get processed by some main auto-regressive model.

articles.title: MCP (MODEL CONTEXT PROTOCOL): SIMPLY EXPLAINED IN 5 MINUTES
articles.summary: MCP (Model Context Protocol) lets AI tools like Claude and ChatGPT integrate with your everyday apps—turning them from isolated chatbots into powerful assistants with real-world capabilities. Instead of copying error logs into your AI, MCP lets you simply say "read my browser console and fix this bug" and the AI can directly access those tools. Behind the scenes, MCP works through a standard interface where providers like Slack, GitHub, and Sentry can create "adapters" that translate AI requests into API calls.

articles.title: FINE-TUNE GEMMA 3 FOR FREE
articles.summary: The Unsloth team has figured out some of the quirks with the new open weights model from DeepMind. By integrating with their toolkit, one can train the model on a free Colab instance.

articles.title: OPENAI'S O1-PRO IS THE COMPANY'S MOST EXPENSIVE AI MODEL YET
articles.summary: OpenAI's o1-pro, a more computing-intensive version of its o1 reasoning AI, is now available in its developer API for select users at a premium cost. Priced at $150 per million input tokens and $600 per million output tokens, it offers better performance but has received mixed early reviews as it struggles with certain tasks. Despite improvements, internal benchmarks show only slight performance gains over the standard o1 model in coding and math.

articles.title: [PODCAST] MACHINE LEARNING AND THE MARKETS: FINDING SIGNAL IN THE NOISE
articles.summary: Jane Street's ML researcher In Young Cho discusses the challenges of machine learning in a low-data, high-noise environment; the shift from

articles.title: linear models to neural networks; and more. Listen here
DEEPSEEK V3-0324 (HUGGING FACE HUB)
articles.summary: DeepSeek has uploaded new model weights to its Hugging Face repository.

articles.title: OPENAI TO START TESTING CHATGPT CONNECTORS FOR GOOGLE DRIVE AND SLACK
articles.summary: OpenAI is beta testing ChatGPT Connectors, enabling business users to link apps like Slack and Google Drive to ChatGPT for informed responses.

articles.title: YC-BACKED REACTWISE IS APPLYING AI TO SPEED UP DRUG MANUFACTURING
articles.summary: ReactWise, a Y Combinator-backed startup, uses AI to accelerate chemical manufacturing, aiming to improve the process by leveraging pre-trained models to reduce trial-and-error.
